title: astragalus lentiginosus var. macdougalii
intro: Astragalus lentiginosus var. macdougalii, also known as MacDougal's milkvetch or MacDougal's desert milkvetch, is a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It belongs to the pea family and has been used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic properties. The medicinal properties of Astragalus lentiginosus var. macdougalii include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are commonly used to alleviate stress, fatigue, and respiratory issues.
